title: Climate Change Resilience in Agriculture: Vulnerability and Adaptation Concerns of Semi-Arid Tropics in Asia
abstract:
There is global consensus that climate is changing mainly due to the anthropogenic
emissions of green house gases (IPCC, 2007b). Also, there is a widespread concern
over long-term climate changes and changes in climate variability through occurrence
of extreme weather events such as cyclones, floods, droughts, sea level rise etc.
Agriculture is exposed, directly and affected considerably by climate and its changes.
Due to the spatially different impacts of climate change, there is a need to understand
its context in each region with respect to exposure, expected impacts, vulnerability status
and the response strategies to mitigate, core and adapt better with the process of climate
change................
